trade.summary: Significant bilateral trade with deep cultural roots (large Italian diaspora in Brazil). Brazil exports soybeans, iron ore, meat, and leather. Italy exports machinery, automotive parts, luxury goods, and fashion.

diplomatic.summary: Exceptionally warm ties rooted in the massive Italian immigration to Brazil (estimated 15-30 million Brazilians of Italian descent). Cultural and educational ties are extensive. Regular high-level visits. Cooperation on environmental and development issues.

military.summary: No military alliance. Some defense industrial contacts. Italy has sold military equipment to Brazil. Joint participation in UN peacekeeping.

financial.summary: Italian companies (Fiat/Stellantis, ENI, UniCredit) have significant operations in Brazil. Stellantis has major automotive manufacturing in Brazil. FDI flows are substantial.

energy.summary: ENI is active in Brazilian offshore oil. Some trade in biofuels. Italy has funded sustainable energy projects in Brazil through development cooperation.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific and cultural ties. Collaboration in architecture, design, food science, and biodiversity. Italian universities have partnerships with Brazilian institutions. Large Italian-Brazilian academic network.
